title: Factories Act 1948 §41B — Compulsory disclosure of information by the occupier
text: Occupier of a factory involving hazardous processes must disclose dangers, health hazards and countermeasures to workers, the Chief Inspector, the local authority and the nearby general public; maintain a health and safety policy; prepare a Chief-Inspector-approved on-site emergency plan and disaster control measures; and lay down approved measures for handling, usage, transport, storage and disposal of hazardous substances, publicised to workers and the public. Failure to notify the Chief Inspector about a hazardous process can lead to licence cancellation.

title: Factories Act 1948 §41C — Specific responsibility of the occupier
text: Occupier must maintain accurate, up-to-date health and medical records of workers exposed to hazardous substances (accessible to the workers), and appoint persons with prescribed qualifications and experience to supervise handling of hazardous substances and ensure protective facilities are provided.

title: Factories Act 1948 §41D — Power of Central Government to appoint Inquiry Committee
text: Central Government may appoint an Inquiry Committee to investigate the standards of health and safety observed in a factory following an extraordinary occurrence involving a hazardous process, and to recommend measures to prevent recurrence.

title: Factories Act 1948 §41F — Permissible limits of exposure of chemical and toxic substances
text: Maximum permissible threshold limits of exposure to chemical and toxic substances in manufacturing processes shall be as laid down in the Second Schedule; the Central Government may amend the Schedule in line with scientific evidence. Exposure beyond the limits requires remedial action.

title: Factories Act 1948 §41H — Right of workers to warn about imminent danger
text: Workers may warn the occupier, agent, manager or person in charge of a hazardous process of reasonable apprehension of imminent danger to their lives or health; the person warned must take immediate remedial action if satisfied, or refer the matter to the nearest Inspector, whose decision is binding.

title: OISD-STD-105 (Rev. I, Sep 2004) — Work Permit System
text: Hydrocarbon processing and handling installations must operate a formal work permit system covering cold work, hot work / confined-space entry, and electrical isolation/energisation. Permits specify gas-test results, isolation, PPE and authorised signatories before work starts, to prevent injury, property damage and fire.
